LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE COURT MODIFIED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT JONATHAN TEO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT TEO'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. THE CONTRACT IS AT LEAST REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE TO PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION THAT MR. TEO IS INDIVIDUALLY LIABLE ON THE CONTRACT. THE COURT ACCEPTS PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION OF THE AGREEMENT AT THIS STAGE OF THE LITIGATION. (SEE COMPLAINT, 27; SEE ARAGON-HAAS V. FAMILY SECURITY INS. SERVICES, INC. (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 232, 239 ["WHILE PLAINTIFFS' INTERPRETATION OF THE CONTRACT MAY PROVE INVALID, IT [IS] IMPROPER TO RESOLVE THE ISSUE AGAINST HER SOLELY ON HER OWN PLEADING. 'IN RULING ON A DEMURRER, THE LIKELIHOOD THAT THE PLEADING WILL BE ABLE TO PROVE HIS ALLEGATIONS IS NOT THE QUESTION.'"].) THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT THE AGREEMENT DID NOT BECOME EFFECTIVE BECAUSE THE "EMPLOYER GROUP" DID NOT EXECUTE IT. BINARY CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC EXECUTED THE AGREEMENT ON BEHALF OF THE "EMPLOYER GROUP." THE DEMURRER TO THE BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE COMPLAINT HAS SUFFICIENT ALLEGATIONS REGARDING MR. TEO'S HIGH DEGREE OF CONTROL AND HE RAISES A FACTUAL ISSUE. (COMPLAINT, 10, 30-31, 36, 56-62.) THE DEMURRER TO THE FRAUD OR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD FACTS REGARDING THE SPECIFIC MISREPRESENTATIONS. THE DEMURRER TO THE CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD OR CONCEALMENT CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DEMURRER TO THE CONVERSION CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT MR. TEO INTERFERED WITH PLAINTIFF'S POSSESSORY RIGHT. (SEE DUKE V. SUPERIOR COURT (2017) 18 CAL.APP.5TH 490, 506 ["IF A DEFENDANT IS AUTHORIZED TO MAKE A SPECIFIC USE OF A PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY, USE IN EXCESS OF THAT AUTHORIZED MAY SUBJECT THE DEFENDANT TO LIABILITY FOR CONVERSION, IF SU

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jay Rapaport, Esq.; Erin Meyer, Esq.; Anjali Srinivasan, Esq. of Keker Van Nest & Peters LLP, Tel: 415.391.5400, for defendant and moving party Jonathan Teo;
Adrian Sawyer, Esq. of Sawyer & Labar, Tel: 415.262.3820, for plaintiff and opposing party;
William Ashworth, Esq.; Vidya Atre Mirmira, Esq.; Matthew Underwood, Esq. of Williams Connolly LLP, Tel: 202.434.5683, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT JONATHAN TEO'S Special Motion To Strike Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16, as follows:

Defendant Jonathan Teo's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the allegations that Teo breached the separation agreement by making disparaging remarks about plaintiff is granted. Teo is entitled to an order striking the allegations as to him, and the fact that the other Binary entities have not moved for relief does not change the analysis. (See Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376, 394-396 [defendant may strike discrete allegations regarding petitioning activity without defeating entire cause of action].)

(See word minutes for the Court's full ruling.)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew Borden, Esq. of BraunHagey & Borden LLP, Tel: 415.599.0212, for defendant and moving party;
Adrian Sawyer, Esq. of Sawyer & Labar, Tel: 415.262.3820, for plaintiff and opposing party;
William Ashworth, Esq.; Vidya Atre Mirmira, Esq.; Matthew Underwood, Esq. of Williams Connolly LLP, Tel: 202.434.5683, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT BINARY CAPITAL MTGP I, LP, Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings as follows:

The motion for judgment on the pleadings of Binary Capital MTGP I, LP is denied.

Even if the court takes judicial notice of the corporate documents, there is a factual issue regarding what the corporate directors did or did not do and whether there was compliance with the charters. The complaint sufficiently alleges that Mr. Teo could act for the Binary entities and the court takes those allegations as true at this point. (Complaint, 23, 25, 27-28.)

Movants similarly fail to establish (1) that Cayman Islands law applies; and (2) plaintiff cannot maintain an action against MTGP 1. This argument again raises factual and choice of law issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer. Even under movant's interpretation of Cayman Islands law, a limited partner such as plaintiff can maintain an action when the general partner unreasonably refuses to do so. (See Movants' points and authorities, pg. 9, fn.5.)

The court rejects movants' argument that the agreement did not become effective because the "Employer Group" did not execute it. Binary Capital Management LLC executed the agreement on behalf of the "Employer Group."

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jay Rapaport, Esq.; Erin Meyer, Esq.; Anjali Srinivasan, Esq. of Keker Van Nest & Peters LLP, Tel: 415.391.5400, for defendant and moving party Jonathan Teo;
Adrian Sawyer, Esq. of Sawyer & Labar, Tel: 415.262.3820, for plaintiff and opposing party;
William Ashworth, Esq.; Vidya Atre Mirmira, Esq.; Matthew Underwood, Esq. of Williams Connolly LLP, Tel: 202.434.5683, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court modified the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT JONATHAN TEO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as to the 3rd cause of action, as follows:

Defendant Teo's demurrer to the complaint is overruled as to the breach of contract cause of action. The contract is at least reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's interpretation that Mr. Teo is individually liable on the contract. The Court accepts plaintiff's interpretation of the agreement at this stage of the litigation. (See Complaint, 27; see Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ["While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleading will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'"].) The Court rejects defendant's argument that the agreement did not become effective because the "Employer Group" did not execute it. Binary Capital Management LLC executed the agreement on behalf of the "Employer Group."

The demurrer to the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action is overruled. The complaint has sufficient allegations regarding Mr. Teo's high degree of control and he raises a factual issue. (Complaint, 10, 30-31, 36, 56-62.)

The demurrer to the fraud or intentional misrepresentation cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead facts regarding the specific misrepresentations.

The demurrer to the constructive fraud or concealment cause of action is sustained with leave to amend.

The demurrer to the conversion cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that Mr. Teo interfered with plaintiff's possessory right. (See Duke v. Superior Court (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 490, 506 ["If a defendant is authorized to make a specific use of a plaintiff's property, use in excess of that authorized may subject the defendant to liability for conversion, if such use seriously violates another's right to control the use of the property."]; Complaint, 70-73.)

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the court's ruling as modified and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
